title: 10 best herbal teas for tickling throat

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol, help combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause sore throats, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, like flavonoids and terpenes, soothe and reduce swelling in the throat, alleviating tickling sensations and pain.
Expectorant properties: Thyme's expectorant qualities help loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits associated with a tickly throat.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, teas helps with tickling throat because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have decongestant and expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, providing relief from tickling and discomfort. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the tickling sensation, promoting a healthy and clear throat.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, thereby reducing tickling and discomfort.
Carvacrol: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, which help combat underlying infections causing the tickling throat, and also has anti-inflammatory effects to soothe the throat.
Rosmarinic acid: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, thereby alleviating the tickling sensation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are used to make teas for tickling the throat because of their high content of thymol, a compound with antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are used to make teas for tickling the throat due to their calming and soothing effects, which can help to reduce throat irritation.
Buds: Thymus vulgaris buds are used to make teas for tickling the throat because they contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which can help to soothe and calm the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 tablespoons of fresh or dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the early morning.
Rinse the harvested leaves and flowers with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the harvested material in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids to prevent any bitterness.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before serving it hot or iced.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Herbal peppermint tea helps to relieve tickling sensations in the throat by relaxing the muscles and reducing spasms, allowing for smoother breathing and swallowing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The menthol present in peppermint oil reduces inflammation in the throat, which can help alleviate discomfort and soothe irritation caused by a tickly throat.
Expectorant properties: Peppermint tea helps to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion, which can exacerbate a tickly throat.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, teas helps with tickling throat because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol content in peppermint helps to numb the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint tea has a soothing and calming effect on the mucous membranes, which helps to reduce inflammation and ease the tickling sensation in the throat. This makes peppermint tea a popular remedy for soothing a tickly throat and relieving congestion.
remedy_constituents: Menthone: Acts as a local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to numb and soothe the irritated tissues in the throat, reducing discomfort and tickling sensations.

Menthol: Exhibits expectorant and decongestant properties, helping to thin mucus and promote its removal from the respiratory tract, thereby reducing the tickling sensation caused by mucus accumulation in the throat.

Rosmarinic Acid: Displays potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the throat, thereby alleviating tickling and discomfort associated with throat irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most widely used part of Peppermint, as they contain a high concentration of menthol, which is responsible for the cooling sensation and decongestant properties.
Stems: The stems are also used, as they contain menthol and menthone, which help to soothe and calm the throat, making them suitable for teas.
Buds: The buds are used for their high menthol content, which helps to provide relief from throat irritation and tickling sensations, making them a popular choice for teas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for best flavor.
Rinse the leaves with cool water to remove dirt and debris and pat dry with a paper towel.
Steep one teaspoon of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in boiling water for three to five minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine mesh sieve into a cup to remove the leaves completely.
Serve the tea immediately and drink within 10 to 15 minutes for optimal flavor and benefits.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antimicrobial properties, which help to fight off infections that can cause tickling or scratchy sensations in the throat, while also providing a protective barrier against further infection.
Saponins: These natural foaming agents help to thin and loosen mucus in the throat, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion, which can contribute to a tickling or uncomfortable sensation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, teas helps with tickling throat because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat. This reduces inflammation and irritation, providing relief from tickling, itching, and discomfort. Liquiritigenin: Liquiritigenin is a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the throat and reduce inflammation, providing relief from a tickling throat.
Licoricidin: Licoricidin is a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which may help reduce inflammation and combat viral infections that can cause a tickling throat.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that gives licorice root its distinct flavor and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps soothe a sore throat.
Leaves: They have a similar composition to the roots and contain glycyrrhizin, which provides similar benefits for a tickling throat.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra may also be used to make tea, although it is less common, and contain compounds that help with inflammation and soothing the throat.
remedy_recipe: Rinse 5-7 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with filtered water for 5 minutes to remove any impurities.
Steep the rinsed roots in 250ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a fine mesh sieve into a cup to separate the roots from the tea.
Add honey to taste to sweeten the tea and enhance its flavor and aroma.
Allow the tea to cool for 10-15 minutes before serving to prevent burns and promote relaxation.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Fennel tea helps to relax the muscles in the throat and reduce spasms, providing relief from tickling or discomfort caused by irritation.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Foeniculum vulgare leaves are commonly used in teas for tickling throat due to their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Foeniculum vulgare seeds are used in teas for tickling throat as they contain anethole, a compound that helps to relax the muscles and soothe the throat.
Flowers: Foeniculum vulgare flowers are used in teas for tickling throat due to their antispasmodic properties, which help to calm and relax the muscles in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare seeds or roots in the early morning after dew has fallen naturally.
Dry foeniculum vulgare seeds or roots for 7 to 10 days in a warm dry place.
Crush dried foeniculum vulgare seeds or roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 1 teaspoon of foeniculum vulgare powder in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids then serve warm or chilled immediately.

remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Lemon verbena's antiviral compounds help combat viral infections that can cause a tickling or scratchy throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers from a healthy plant in the morning when they are at their peak potency.

Combine 1 teaspoon of dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers with 1 cup of boiling water to make a strong infusion.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes to release the active compounds and essential oils.
Strain the tea and discard the solids then serve hot or iced depending on your preference.

Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to infuse.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers or 2 to 3 teaspoons of fresh flowers.

Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the flowers.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger root and wash it thoroughly under cold running water.
Peel the ginger using a vegetable peeler or a sharp knife cutting away any blemishes.
Slice the peeled ginger into thin coins weighing approximately 5 grams per cup of water.
Boil one cup of pure filtered water in a tea kettle or pot and pour it over the sliced ginger.
Steep for 5-7 minutes allowing the spicy oils to infuse into the hot water before straining.

Additionally, turmeric's natural antibacterial properties help combat infections that can cause tickling sensations. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat, providing relief from tickling sensations.

Antioxidant property: Turmeric tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the mucous membranes in the throat from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to throat irritation and tickling.
Expectorant property: Turmeric tea contains compounds that help loosen and clear mucus from the throat, making it easier to expel phlegm and reducing congestion and tickling sensations.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These underground stems have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in soothing a tickling throat.
Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain curcumin, a potent antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and combat infection in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Boil one teaspoon of dried turmeric powder in two cups of water for seven minutes.
Add one tablespoon of grated fresh ginger and allow it to simmer for five minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a teapot or cup.
Discard the solids and let the tea cool down to room temperature within thirty seconds.
Store the cooled tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass teas helps with tickling throat because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the irritated tissues in the throat. The citrusy flavor and aroma of lemongrass also help to relax the muscles in the esophagus, reducing spasms that can cause discomfort and tickling sensations. Additionally, lemongrass contains antioxidants and compounds like citral and geraniol, which have natural antibacterial properties, helping to combat any underlying infections or irritants that may be contributing to the tickling sensation.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemongrass tea's antimicrobial compounds, such as citral and geraniol, help combat bacteria and viruses in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with tickling sensations.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in lemongrass tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, providing relief from tickling sensations.

Expectorant properties: Lemongrass tea's expectorant compounds, such as citral and limonene, help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion that can contribute to tickling in the throat.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, lemongrass roots help soothe and calm an itchy throat.
Rhizomes: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, rhizomes effectively reduce swelling and discomfort caused by a tickly throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the morning when dew is still present and rinse thoroughly with clean water.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried or 2 teaspoons of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves with 1 cup of boiling filtered water for steeping.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on personal preference for tea strength and flavor.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the herb particles and discard solids.
Enjoy the soothing tea immediately while still warm, ideally within 30 minutes of preparation to maximize its benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with tickling throat because it contains mucilages, which are natural soothing agents that calm and protect the mucous membranes. As we drink the tea, the mucilages coat the throat and chest areas, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by dryness or irritation. Additionally, marshmallow root has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease congestion, allowing for a more comfortable and relaxed swallowing experience.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling in the throat and alleviate discomfort caused by coughing or tickling sensations.

Expectorant properties: Marshmallow tea has expectorant properties that help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up phlegm and relieving congestion and tickliness in the throat.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, which helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, making it an effective remedy for tickling throat.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the marshmallow plant are rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried marshmallow root and boil it in two cups of water for 10-15 minutes.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let it steep for an additional five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids.
Add one tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea if desired, and stir well to combine.
Serve the warm tea immediately and allow it to cool to room temperature before storing in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with tickling throat because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in rosemary leaves have been shown to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with a tickly throat. Additionally, rosemary's antimicrobial properties help combat viral and bacterial infections that can cause sore throats, allowing the body to heal faster and more effectively. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary tea's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the throat, which can alleviate tickling sensations caused by irritation or infection.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and borneol, can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat, providing relief from tickling and coughing.

Expectorant properties: The saponins present in rosemary tea can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and relieving tickling sensations caused by postnasal drip.

Seeds: Rosemary seeds contain antioxidants and flavonoids that aid in reducing inflammation and relieving congestion in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves from reputable sources in sufficient quantity for brewing.
Measure one teaspoon of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in boiling water for five to seven minutes to allow active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard solids after three to five minutes.
Allow the tea to cool for two to three minutes before consuming, then sip slowly to help soothe tickling throat sensations.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Agrimony teas helps with tickling throat because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The tea contains flavonoids and saponins, which help to reduce swelling in the throat and alleviate discomfort. When consumed as a warm tea, it provides instant relief from tickling sensations and helps to loosen phlegm, making it an effective remedy for soothing a scratchy throat.

Expectorant properties: Agrimony's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the throat, allowing for easier breathing and alleviating congestion that can contribute to tickling sensations.
Antimicrobial properties: Agrimony tea's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and viral infections in the throat, which can cause inflammation, irritation, and tickling sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Agrimony roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm an irritated throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Barks: The barks of the agrimony plant are rich in antioxidants and tannins, which help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the throat, providing relief from tickling sensations.
remedy_recipe: Gather agrimonia eupatoria leaves and flowers in sufficient quantities for brewing.
Measure out two to three teaspoons of dried herbal mixture per cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture in boiling water for five to seven minutes to allow for optimal infusion.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot.
Drink the warm tea immediately and repeat as needed throughout the day.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to tickling throat that are treated with herbal teas are conditions like sore throats, coughs, and hoarseness. Herbal teas such as slippery elm, marshmallow root, and licorice root soothe irritated mucous membranes and reduce inflammation, providing relief from discomfort and congestion. Additionally, teas containing honey, eucalyptus, and peppermint may also help to calm a tickly throat and ease respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for tickling throat are natural remedies that help soothe and calm an irritated throat, providing relief from discomfort and inflammation. The best herbal teas for tickling throat include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Thymus vulgaris, Echinacea purpurea, Sambucus nigra, Eucalyptus globulus, Rosmarinus officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Mentha x piperita, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, tickling throat can lead to loss of sleep, difficulty concentrating, and decreased productivity, ultimately impacting one's quality of life and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with tickling throat is a blend of soothing and antibacterial properties. Slipping elm and licorice root teas work together to calm inflammation and reduce throat irritation. Adding a pinch of thyme and lemon balm tea enhances the antibacterial properties, helping to combat underlying infections. This balanced blend of herbal teas provides a natural and effective way to soothe a tickling throat, promoting relaxation and overall well-being.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of Alternative and Complementary Medicine (New York, N.Y.), herbal teas like Throat Coat, a traditional demulcent tea, can be effective in soothing a tickling throat due to their demulcent properties, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes. The study, a multicenter, prospective, randomized, double-blinded, placebo-controlled clinical trial, involved 60 patients with acute pharyngitis, who were randomly assigned to receive either Throat Coat or a placebo. The results showed that Throat Coat significantly reduced the intensity of throat pain when swallowing, with a 43.8% decrease in the sum of pain intensity differences (SPID) compared to a 16.5% decrease in the placebo group, indicating that herbal teas like Throat Coat can provide rapid, temporary relief of sore throat pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas helps with tickling throat because of its soothing properties, particularly its licorice root content, which contains glycyrrhizin. This compound has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, reducing inflammation and mucus production in the throat. Additionally, the tea's sweet and slightly bitter taste helps calm the throat, providing instant relief from tickling sensations. The tea's warm and comforting nature further aids in relaxing the throat muscles, offering a gentle and natural solution to soothe a tickling throat.
remedy_constituents: Liquiritin: This phenolic glycoside has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the throat, providing relief from tickling sensations.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic effects, which help alleviate irritation and itching in the throat, reducing tickling sensations.

Isoflavones: These polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the throat from damage and reduce inflammation, providing relief from tickling and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice root is the most commonly used part of Glycyrrhiza glabra to make teas for tickling the throat, as it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that soothes and calms the mucous membranes.
Barks: Licorice bark is also used to make teas for tickling the throat, as it contains similar compounds to the roots that help to reduce inflammation and irritation.
Leaves: Licorice leaves are sometimes used to make teas for tickling the throat, as they contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin and other compounds that provide similar soothing benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Wash the roots thoroughly with cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the roots into small pieces and dry them in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its active compounds.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas helps with tickling throat because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe irritated tissues. The active compound, gingerol, has been shown to reduce inflammation and calm the mucous membranes in the throat, providing relief from tickling and discomfort. Additionally, the tea's warm and spicy flavor helps to loosen mucus and promote drainage, allowing the throat to heal and recover more efficiently. This makes Zingiber officinale teas a popular remedy for soothing a tickling throat.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help in reducing inflammation and soothing the mucous membranes in the throat, providing relief from tickling sensations.
Volatile oils (primarily Gingerene and Shogaol): These oils have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help in numbing the pain and discomfort associated with a tickling throat.

Gingerols' analogues, Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols possess anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing swelling and discomfort in the throat, thereby alleviating the tickling sensation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they contain gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the throat.
Leaves: Leaves are used because they have a mild flavor and contain compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy throat.
Roots: Roots are used because they contain a high concentration of gingerols, which help reduce pain and inflammation in the throat, providing relief from tickling sensations.
remedy_recipe: Dry the fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in a single layer at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 2-3 grams of dried zingiber officinale into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Steep the ground zingiber officinale in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release flavors.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids immediately.
Serve the herbal tea warm or at room temperature and enjoy within 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas helps with tickling throat because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes. The antimicrobial compounds present in Thymus vulgaris, such as thymol and carvacrol, effectively combat infections and reduce inflammation, providing quick relief from tickling sensations. Additionally, the warm, comforting brew can help to loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to swallow and relieving discomfort associated with tickling throats. This makes Thymus vulgaris teas a popular remedy for soothing throat irritations.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as an antibacterial and antifungal agent, reducing inflammation and microbial activity that may contribute to tickling sensations in the throat.
Carvacrol: Exhibits decongestant properties, helping to reduce mucus production and inflammation in the respiratory tract, which may alleviate tickling throat sensations.
Rosmarinic acid: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with tickling throat sensations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of thymol, a compound known for its antimicrobial properties and soothing effects on the throat.
Flowers: Used for their pleasant fragrance and ability to provide relief from sore throats due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Used because they contain a significant amount of thymol, similar to the leaves, which helps in soothing and calming a sore throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 ounces of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested leaves and flowers in a single layer for 2-3 hours at 95-105 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of dried thymus vulgaris with 1 cup of boiling water to create a strong tea infusion.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes before straining the liquid through a fine mesh sieve.
Consume the tea within 24 hours and store the leftover herbal infusion in the refrigerator at 40-41 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas helps with tickling throat because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to boost the immune system. The tea's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, help to reduce swelling and soothe the mucous membranes in the throat. This can provide quick relief from tickling and discomfort, making it an effective remedy for those experiencing sore throats. Regular consumption of Echinacea purpurea tea may also help prevent future occurrences.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: They help to reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the throat, providing relief from tickling and discomfort.
Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): As a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, quercetin helps to reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, alleviating tickling sensations.

Caffeic acid: This phenolic acid has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to combat infections and reduce inflammation in the throat, providing relief from tickling and soreness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help soothe and calm the throat.
Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the throat.
Roots: Rich in polysaccharides that stimulate the immune system and help fight off infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 ounces of fresh or dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in late summer or early fall.
Rinse the roots and flowers with cool water to remove dirt and debris from the harvesting process.
Combine the rinsed roots and flowers in a 1:1 ratio and chop them into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the chopped roots and flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra teas helps with tickling throat because it contains high levels of antiviral and antimicrobial compounds, specifically sambucol. These properties help soothe and calm the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The tea's expectorant properties also aid in loosening and clearing mucus, making it easier to cough up and relieve tickling sensations. remedy_recipe: Gather sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in a quantity of 1 teaspoon dried per 8 ounce cup of water.
Heat 8 ounces of water to a boil then reduce heat to a simmer for 5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves to the simmering water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine mesh sieve into a cup to remove loose particles.
Allow the tea to steep for an additional 2 minutes then serve immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas helps with tickling throat because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's primary compound, eucalyptol, acts as a natural expectorant, loosening mucus and phlegm, making it easier to cough up. This helps to clear out irritants and reduce throat inflammation, providing relief from tickling and discomfort. The tea's soothing and cooling sensation also helps to calm the throat, providing quick and effective relief from tickling and coughing.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with tickling.

Quercetin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and irritation in the throat, which can contribute to a tickling sensation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the most commonly used part for teas due to their high concentration of volatile oils, particularly eucalyptol, which provides decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Eucalyptus buds are used for teas because they contain a higher concentration of volatile oils than the leaves, making them more effective for relieving respiratory issues.
Stems: Eucalyptus stems are used for teas due to the presence of eucalyptol and other volatile oils that help to reduce congestion and soothe the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves from a reputable supplier or your own garden.
Rinse the dried leaves in a fine mesh strainer under cold running water for 30 seconds.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the dried leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine mesh strainer into a cup or teapot to remove the leaves.
Serve the tea hot or cold and enjoy its refreshing and decongestant properties immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas helps with tickling throat because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the irritated mucous membranes in the throat. remedy_recipe: Choose high-quality fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves preferably organic and free of pesticides.
Measure 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves or 2-3 teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Heat 8 ounces of water to a boil for 5 minutes in a tea kettle.
Steep the rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves then serve immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas helps with tickling throat because of its soothing and calming properties. remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe an irritated throat.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 tablespoon of dried flowers from another plant is not to be used.
Boil 8 ounces of water for 2 minutes and then reduce heat to a simmer.
Pour the boiling water over the dried flowers in a teapot and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids.
Drink the tea immediately and enjoy the calming effects of lavandula angustifolia within 10-15 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita teas helps with tickling throat because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol content in peppermint tea provides a cooling sensation that helps to calm the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort. The antispasmodic properties also help to relax the muscles in the throat, thereby reducing coughing and tickling sensations. Additionally, peppermint tea's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus, further relieving a tickling throat. remedy_constituents: Menthol: It helps to numb the throat and provide temporary relief from tickling sensations by blocking the nerve endings that transmit the sensation.

Rosmarinic acid: It acts as an antispasmodic agent, helping to relax the muscles in the throat and prevent spasms that can cause tickling sensations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Mint leaves are commonly used to make teas for tickling the throat due to their cooling and soothing properties.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves or 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Steep the gathered leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove loose particles.
Add honey to taste to sweeten the tea if desired for flavor.

remedy_recipe: Select high-quality dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a reputable source ensuring freshness and potency.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves for every 8 ounces of water.
Combine the measured ginkgo biloba leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water in a tea infuser or strainer.
